unitTitle: Unit 16: Materials and the Built Environment
title: Materials and the Built Environment

phrasal_verbs.meaning: cover a window or door with wooden boards
lock the doors of a building or business
be removed from something such as clothing or cloth by washing or rubbing; have a particular result or end in a particular way
remove something from a larger piece by cutting; stop eating something or doing something, especially because it is bad for your health
if something piles up or someone piles it up, the amount of it increases a lot
stop something from falling by putting something under it or against it; help a government, system, organisation, etc continue to exist, especially by providing financial or military support
fix something such as equipment in the place where it will be used and make it ready to use; make an official request, claim, offer, etc
make something by joining all its parts; produce or organise something using many different things; choose people or things to form a team or group
build something such as a wall, fence or house; let someone stay in your house
build a structure or put it in a particular place; make a piece of equipment ready for use
if people in a group spread out, they move away from one another so that they cover a large area
separate a large structure into pieces; write down information or a statement
add water to a drink or liquid in order to make it less strong, dilute; make something such as a statement or newspaper article less offensive, powerful or detailed
make something gradually disappear or become thinner by using or rubbing it; make someone gradually lose their energy or confidence

idioms.meaning: something that is ugly and spoils the appearance of a place
a city or area that has a lot of large ugly buildings and no trees or plants
behave in a relaxed way, as if you were in your own home
go to places of entertainment, such as bars and restaurants, in the evening
the area around a city where many people live who travel to the city to work
living in the countryside, far from towns or cities
a small town that regularly has a market and acts as a centre for the surrounding area
a place where you feel as relaxed and comfortable as in your own home
become homeless and have nowhere to live
said when you return to your home and are happy to be there

B.title: Complete using the words in bold in the correct form
B.instructions: Complete the passage using the words in bold in the correct form.
B.passage: What prevents a heavy object from sliding down a rough slope? The answer lies in (1) ...... , the resistance created when two surfaces press against each other. When two (2) ...... come into contact, the irregularities in each surface lock together, making movement difficult. However, if a layer of (3) ...... forms between the surfaces, this resistance is greatly reduced. At that point, the pull of (4) ...... takes over, and the object begins to slide.

(2) When two ...... come into contact

C.title: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box (The built environment)
C.instructions: Complete the text using the correct form of the words provided.
C.passage: The planned (1) ...... around the historic city of Sandford has reignited controversy among residents. Local planners have spent six years trying to (2) ...... the new route in order to ease congestion in the (3) ...... , one of the most densely (4) ...... areas in the entire region. D.title: Circle the correct word
D.instructions: Circle the correct word to complete each sentence.

questions.sentence: I find it hard to breathe when I spend too much time in such a **built-up / high-rise** area with no green spaces.
My uncle recently moved into a **built-up / high-rise** block on the fourteenth floor with spectacular views across the city.
The very first **skyline / skyscraper** was erected in Chicago, marking a turning point in the history of urban architecture.
From the observation deck, visitors could take in the breathtaking **skyline / skyscraper** stretching across the horizon.
The charity is working hard to provide affordable **estate / housing** for vulnerable families across the city.
They grew up together on the same **estate / housing** on the edge of town and have been friends ever since.
After the fire, the council took the decision to **demolish / evict** the damaged block of flats.
The landlord tried to **demolish / evict** the tenants without giving them the required notice.
The old lighthouse is a remarkable **structure / infrastructure** that has withstood centuries of harsh weather.
Developing nations often lack the **structure / infrastructure** necessary to support rapid economic growth.

E.title: Write one word in each gap (Phrasal verbs in context)
E.instructions: Write one word in each gap to complete the text.
E.passage: Since the tourist industry collapsed in the area, it has become a common sight to see shops that have been boarded (1) ...... along the seafront. Many restaurants and cafés have closed (2) ...... permanently, and the problems of urban decline have been piling (3) ...... for some time. Nevertheless, the local authority has finally decided to fix the area (4) ...... and attract new visitors and businesses. The plan involves knocking (5) ...... derelict warehouses near the harbour and putting (6) ...... modern mixed-use developments in their place. Old billboards advertising long-closed businesses are being taken (7) ...... , and new cycle paths and family-friendly spaces are being put (8) ...... along the promenade.

closed (2) ...... permanently
piling (3) ...... for some time
fix the area (4) ......
knocking (5) ...... derelict warehouses
putting (6) ...... modern mixed-use developments
billboards are being taken (7) ......
are being put (8) ...... along the promenade

I.title: Write one word in each gap
I.instructions: Write one word in each gap to complete the text.
I.passage: I've always kept a sharp eye (1) ...... architectural trends, so when the time came to design my own home, I was determined to create something truly different. It was far from simple. Planning regulations appeared to block (2) ...... path at every turn and, to (3) ...... matters worse, finding an architect who shared my vision took far longer than I had anticipated. A contact from work gave me a window (4) ...... the world of architecture and introduced me to someone who had handled similar projects. Was I being unrealistic? That, some might argue, is a (5) ...... of opinion. But beneath the appearance I wanted practicality – (6) ...... the surface, everything needed to be energy-efficient and future-proof. Countless hours of research smoothed the (7) ...... considerably. I took part in the physical work personally, helping to bend metal framework (8) ...... shape and laying some of the internal flooring myself. I'd gladly erect a monument (9) ...... the engineer who developed the solar heating system we installed. As it turned out, all the sceptics' warnings of failure (10) ...... no foundation and the project was completed in under a year.

M.title: Word formation
M.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of the line to form a word that fits in the space in the same line.
M.passage: By the early 1960s, (1) ...... could already see that the industrial waterfront district of the city of Grayvale was in urgent need of transformation. Initial proposals had to be (2) ...... when a period of economic instability gripped the region. Warehouses and old factory buildings had been (4) ...... damaged, making any form of restoration impossible. Aiming to create a far more (5) ...... environment for the city's (6) ...... to enjoy, a team of town planners put forward a bold new vision. By pedestrianising the waterfront and introducing parks, they hoped to make the district more (7) ...... and attractive to families. There were (8) ...... from local property developers who feared disruption to their existing projects, but the plans moved forward regardless. What had once been a (9) ...... packed industrial zone gradually became a vibrant and welcoming neighbourhood. The result was a remarkable (10) ...... triumph that inspired similar schemes in cities across the country.

questions.sentence: (3) ...... (MATERIAL)
(10) ...... (ARCHITECT)
